trigger.reason: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a new Mythos-class model available to the public, meeting all trigger criteria: top-tier lab source, new model release, and likely to draw broad market attention and sympathy-fade selling in adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
primary_event.summary: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology. This is a significant capability escalation following Anthropic's recent warning about AI dangers.

secondary_events.headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release — a Mythos-class public model with cybersecurity/biology guardrails — for sympathy-fade impact across edge-AI semis, vertical SaaS, cybersecurity, healthcare automation, and municipal services names.
discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape — VRRM rallied (no fade to grade), AMBA and TENB sold off on a thematic Anthropic blast that doesn't map cleanly to their physical/regulated moats, NCNO actually rose, and OMCL ticked down modestly. The cleanest sympathy-fade BUY candidates are AMBA (edge-AI beneficiary mis-tagged as a victim) and TENB (cyber demand likely rises with AI-generated exploit proliferation, per their own 10-Q language).

discoveries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly positions Ambarella as an AI beneficiary: 'Most of our SoCs... support AI functionality... Our latest generation of products also enable us to address computationally intense AI applications for deep fusion, deep planning, multi-modal vision-language models (VLMs), large language models (LLMs), vision-action models (VLAs) and reasoning models in edge devices.' Claude Fable 5 is a cloud-hosted LLM; it does not compete with edge-inference SoCs for cameras, automotive, or robotics.
discoveries.thesis: Ambarella sells edge-AI inference silicon — cameras, automotive ADAS, robotics — into physical devices where Claude (a cloud LLM accessed via API) cannot operate. The sell-off treats AMBA as a generic 'AI-adjacent semi' but the filings show it's actually positioned as a TAM beneficiary of every new VLM/LLM/VLA workload that needs to run on-device. The week's extended drawdown looks like indiscriminate semi de-risking layered on top of today's Anthropic sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued edge-AI design win announcements in robotics/automotive; commentary from Mobileye, NVIDIA Jetson on edge inference demand growth; any explicit Anthropic/edge partnership narrative.
discoveries.what_kills: A guide-down tied to Insta360/WT concentration (70% of revenue through one distributor is a real, non-AI risk); China export-control escalation; evidence that hyperscaler-hosted models are displacing on-device inference in target markets.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Edge-AI inference silicon is a beneficiary, not a victim, of new frontier model releases — more capable models drive more on-device workloads. Sympathy-fade screens should flag this category as a recurring false-victim pattern when frontier labs ship.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AMBA carries the 'AI semi' tag and gets swept into AI-event de-risking flows despite Anthropic shipping a cloud LLM, not an edge-inference chip competitor.
research_pointers: Check WT Microelectronics concentration trajectory — 70% of revenue is the real risk, not Anthropic
Review most recent earnings commentary on automotive design-win pipeline and robotics traction
Compare today's move to peers (NVDA, QCOM edge-AI commentary, MBLY) to isolate sympathy vs. fundamental
Look for any China export restriction updates that could explain the 5-day drawdown independently

discoveries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-Q explicitly frames AI as a demand tailwind: 'Advances in artificial intelligence, including models capable of rapidly identifying and exploiting software vulnerabilities, may further increase the speed and effectiveness of cyberattacks. As a result, vulnerabilities or flaws in our products, systems or those of third parties upon which we rely may be discovered more quickly...' and 'As cyber threats increasingly leverage artificial intelligence... customer expectations for rapid identification, prioritization and remediation of vulnerabilities continue to increase.' Tenable also already ships ExposureAI and Tenable AI Assistant.
discoveries.thesis: Anthropic shipped Fable 5 with explicit cybersecurity guardrails — the opposite of an offensive cyber capability that would displace Tenable. Tenable's continuous-scan agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure is a data-collection layer, not a knowledge-retrieval product an LLM substitutes for. Filings explicitly state AI-accelerated exploit discovery is a tailwind for vulnerability management demand. The week's drawdown looks like generic cyber-SaaS de-risking on the Anthropic headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer cyber names (RPD, QLYS, CRWD) rebounding while AI thematic settles; commentary at upcoming earnings on AI-driven attack surface expansion; new exposure-management bookings.
discoveries.what_kills: Government-customer funding cuts (DOGE-related, explicitly cited in 10-Q); a specific competitor product launch built on a frontier model; evidence that Anthropic-designated 'supply chain risk' (mentioned in TENB's own 10-Q re: federal designations) materially crimps their AI roadmap.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When the trigger event includes safety guardrails on cyber/bio capabilities, cybersecurity vendor sell-offs are particularly mispriced — the lab is explicitly NOT shipping the offensive capability that would commoditize the defender. Track this pattern.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Tenable is a cybersecurity SaaS that gets caught in the 'AI replaces SaaS' trade even though the trigger model explicitly blocks cyber use cases and AI-generated exploits structurally drive more demand for Tenable's scanning layer.
research_pointers: Quote the 10-Q AI-tailwind language directly in any thesis write-up — management has handed you the bull case
Monitor federal AI supply-chain designations naming Anthropic — TENB's 10-Q flags this could force re-engineering
Cross-check QLYS, RPD same-day moves to confirm sector-wide sympathy vs. TENB-specific weakness
Watch for any DOGE-related federal contract guidance — this is a real non-AI risk that could be the actual driver

skipped.reason: Stock RALLIED sharply today — no sympathy-fade to grade. Curation hypothesis (physical cameras, municipal contracts as moat) looks correct but irrelevant when there's no panic.

Stock was UP today — no sympathy fade present. Note: filings contain extensive AI-disruption risk language ('Our growth strategy increasingly depends on the adoption and effective use of our AI capabilities by our customers') that partially contradicts the curator's clean false-victim hypothesis; banking-regulated workflow moat is real but AI exposure is more explicit than curation suggests.

Modest sympathy drop but threat assessment is genuinely minimal (physical pharmacy robotics, DEA-regulated cabinets). Move size is small enough and 5-day move muted enough that confidence in an OVERDONE call is only ~3; pass for now and re-engage if drop deepens without company-specific news.
